rationale: The cited paper is used for a specific interpretive role, not just as a background list reference. In the results discussion, the authors explicitly invoke Swain et al. (2008) to label a detected pattern as "misresponse" and contrast prior evidence (misresponse with reversed/nonreversed items) with their own finding (misresponse can also occur without reversed items). This citation helps position the meaning and contribution of one discovered response-style segment.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on Swain et al.'s misresponse concept to interpret its own latent-class findings and argues that the phenomenon extends to surveys without reversed items. Thus, it uses the cited work as a prior context and adds a new empirical setting where similar misresponse appears.
